questions.text: Who is the intended adopter persona? `dist/install.sh` shows how a repo adopts the engine but not who the target is — platform-engineering teams, individual maintainers, an internal AI-platform group? Name the primary adopter persona and their maturity level. (Product Owner)

What are the success metrics? The code carries counters (state_transitions, total_iterations) but no target defining success. What outcome is the product optimizing — reviewer time saved, defect-escape rate, protocol-authoring velocity, adoption count? (Product Owner)

What is the segment / priority ordering across use cases? Six protocols ship; `code-review` and `recover-mental-model` are flagged as 'the real ones'. Which is the flagship, which are experimental, and what is the business priority ordering among them? (Product Owner)

Which quality characteristic has priority? The code shows security, reliability, and maintainability mechanisms at once but not their ranking. Give a top-3 ordering (needed to justify trade-offs like the deliberate security weakening and to fill the Q3.9 Pugh matrices). (Product Owner)

What is the acceptable Actions-minute cost at scale? Each transition is a full four-job workflow run and each iteration re-dispatches. What is the acceptable cost ceiling per PR / per month at production scale? (Product Owner, Operations)

Why the specific LLM endpoint and model pin? The literal LLM endpoint (a Tailscale host) and the `claude-sonnet-4-6` pin are in code, but not the why — why this self-hosted endpoint, why this model tier over alternatives, and what the migration plan is. (Architect)

Which alternatives were weighed for the major decisions? Decisions (invert-control, recursive unification, firewall-off, one-transition-per-run) are recorded but not scored. Which alternatives were actually considered (e.g. long-lived driver vs. one-transition-per-run; firewall-with-proxy vs. firewall-off), and how did they score against the quality priorities? (Architect)

Are the timeouts and poll counts targets or safety caps? Agent `timeout-minutes` (10/30/45/60) and the 24×5s dispatch poll are code-visible. What are the intended budgets — acceptable end-to-end review latency, concurrent-PR throughput — versus mere safety caps? (Architect, Operations)

What is the threat model and compliance scope? Security mechanisms (trust zones, injection-safety) are visible but the target is not. What is the threat model (malicious PR author? compromised LLM endpoint? supply-chain of installed protocols?), and are any compliance frameworks in scope? (Architect, Operations)

What is the trust model for third-party protocols? Checks (sandboxed, zone 3) and publish hooks (trusted, zone 4) are arbitrary executables the engine runs; a publish hook holds the PUBLISH_TOKEN. What is the intended vetting/trust model for installing a protocol authored outside the repo? (Architect)

Who operates this in production and how are incidents handled? The code shows failure modes (failed state, exhausted iterations, CAS conflicts) but not the operational owner, incident handling, or the recovery procedure beyond re-triggering. Needed for arc42 Ch.7/11 completeness. (Operations)
